behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output quality (grammar and meaning preservation). A stubbed translator would always hide this failure mode, so unit/integration/system checks of wiring are insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run real translation API calls on a representative multilingual evaluation set and score outputs for adequacy/fluency (or human-reviewed rubric). Pass only if semantic meaning is preserved and grammar quality meets threshold.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about real external error-path handling (503 contract) and proving externally visible service behavior under that condition. Real HTTP/API error semantics can differ from mocks, so system-level reality check is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end request against the running service with the primary API forced to return real HTTP 503; verify caller still gets 200/success with fallback translation and no propagated translator error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Agentic because behavior text focuses on 'returns a 503 error' and fallback routing, not model output quality.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff between fallback translator and real SQLite data access. Needs real local DB wiring, but not external network/subprocess or AI output judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Seed SQLite with known phrase pairs, invoke fallback path for existing phrases, and assert returned translations exactly match stored rows.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected System because behavior text is limited to local SQLite lookup correctness, with no real external network/subprocess boundary.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Computation plus persistence must be validated across modules (distance calculator + metric storage). No dependence on real external infra or model judgment for correctness of this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Provide deterministic primary/fallback text pairs, run quality tracking pipeline, assert stored edit-distance values match expected calculations for each case.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about deterministic edit-distance computation/storage, not evaluating model output quality.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across service components (primary selection + async quality logging). It can be validated with deterministic translator doubles and real internal persistence/queue wiring; no required dependence on model-quality judgments.
behaviors.test_description: With both translators enabled, call service and assert response uses primary output; then verify background quality-comparison record is persisted within timeout.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Agentic because behavior text checks selection/orchestration and background logging, not whether model translation itself is semantically good.
